genus Papio = chacma baboon in South Africa
Southern lesser galago (Galago moholi) is not in the SpeciesNet taxonomy; mapped to genus Galago
Natal red duiker (only Cephalophus in SA); mapped at genus level
Mapped to genus Genetta; small-spotted/common genet (G. genetta) also occurs in SA but isn't separately listed, so all genets fold here
SpeciesNet lists crested guineafowl under genus Guttera (species G. pucherani, labeled 'pintade huppee')
Red hartebeest (A. buselaphus caama); SpeciesNet has species-level 'hartebeest'
Mapped to bustard family (Otididae); korhaans/other bustards aren't listed and fold here

SURPRISING: lion (Panthera leo) is not on the list but is common in South Africa. Mapping genus Panthera to leopard folds any lion prediction into 'Leopard' - please confirm lions are truly absent from the study area
Selous' mongoose (Paracynictis selousi) not in SpeciesNet; genus Paracynictis absent and family Herpestidae has other listed mongooses, so left unmapped for you to decide
Genus Herpestes; Cape grey (H. pulverulentus) and Egyptian (H. ichneumon) mongooses also occur in SA and would fold into slender mongoose
Water mongoose = marsh mongoose (Atilax paludinosus)
Samango (Cercopithecus albogularis) is not a distinct SpeciesNet category; mapped to genus Cercopithecus (SpeciesNet's nearest species is C. mitis, 'blue monkey')
Cape clawless otter (Aonyx capensis) not in SpeciesNet; mapped to genus Aonyx (clawless otters)
Temminck's ground pangolin (Smutsia temminckii) not in SpeciesNet; mapped to the pangolin family (Manidae), the only pangolin in SA
Genus Ceratotherium (white rhino only); black rhino (Diceros bicornis) is a different genus and will NOT fold here - it would fall through to Mammal_unspecified
Mapped to order Hyracoidea; tree/bush hyrax would fold into rock hyrax
Genus Hippotragus; roan antelope (H. equinus) also occurs in parts of SA and would fold into sable
Genus Lepus; other hares (e.g. Cape hare, L. capensis) fold into scrub hare
Family Soricidae (true shrews); elephant shrews/sengis are a different order and won't fold here
Family Sciuridae (tree and ground squirrels)
Mapped to order Testudines; covers all tortoises/terrapins (chelonian predictions in SA generally roll up to this level)
Maps both 'wild cat' (F. silvestris) and 'african wild cat' (F. silvestris lybica); domestic cat is kept separate as F. catus
SpeciesNet common name for C. taurinus is 'common wildebeest' (= blue wildebeest)
Plains/Burchell's zebra
Mountain zebra also occurs in parts of SA; included so any zebra prediction maps to 'Zebra' - remove this row if mountain zebra is absent from the site
